title: Multiplication Strategies with Multiples of 10
essential_question: How can you use place value and a number line to multiply with multiples of 10?
strategies: Use a number line: make equal jumps of the multiple.
Use place value: rewrite 70 as 7 tens, multiply, convert back.
Use the basic fact first, then add a zero.

learn.title: Number Line Strategy
learn.content: To find 2 × 40, start at 0 and make 2 jumps of 40 on a number line: 0 → 40 → 80. So 2 × 40 = 80.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.5 Lesson 5.4 'Multiplication Strategies for Multiples of 10' pp.201-204
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 3 Topic G — Place Value Strategies for Multiples of 10
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Place Value Strategy
learn.content: Rewrite the multiple of 10 in tens. 5 × 70 = 5 × 7 tens = 35 tens = 350. Use the basic fact (5 × 7 = 35) then think in tens.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.5 Lesson 5.4 'Multiplication Strategies for Multiples of 10' pp.201-204
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 3 Topic G — Place Value Strategies for Multiples of 10
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: The Zero Trick
learn.content: Multiply the non-zero digits: 6 × 4 = 24. Then put back the zero: 6 × 40 = 240. This works because 40 = 4 × 10.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.5 Lesson 5.4 'Multiplication Strategies for Multiples of 10' pp.201-204
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 3 Topic G — Place Value Strategies for Multiples of 10
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Number Line for Bigger Facts
learn.content: You can use a number line for any multiple of 10. For 4 × 30: start at 0 and jump 30 four times: 0 → 30 → 60 → 90 → 120. Count the jumps and you land on 120. So 4 × 30 = 120.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.5 Lesson 5.4 'Multiplication Strategies for Multiples of 10' pp.201-204
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 3 Topic G — Place Value Strategies for Multiples of 10
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: The Rule Always Works
learn.content: For any multiplication with a multiple of 10: A × (B × 10) = (A × B) × 10. Examples: 5 × 40 = (5 × 4) × 10 = 20 × 10 = 200. 9 × 60 = (9 × 6) × 10 = 54 × 10 = 540. Step 1: find the basic fact. Step 2: multiply by 10 (add a zero).

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.5 Lesson 5.4 'Multiplication Strategies for Multiples of 10' pp.201-204
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 3 Topic G — Place Value Strategies for Multiples of 10
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 결합법칙으로 풀기 — 7 × 60의 마술
learn.content: 10의 배수 곱셈은 결합법칙으로 풀면 깔끔합니다. 예: 7 × 60 = 7 × (6 × 10) = (7 × 6) × 10 = 42 × 10 = 420. 단계 1: 60 = 6 × 10으로 풀어쓰기. 단계 2: 7 × 6 = 42 (기본 사실 — 외운 곱셈표). 단계 3: 42 × 10 = 420 (0 한 개 붙이기). 이 3단계가 머릿속에서는 1초만에 진행됨. 흔한 실수: 결합법칙 없이 답만 외우려다 오류 (M01·M02).

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.5 Lesson 5.4 'Multiplication Strategies for Multiples of 10' pp.201-204
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 3 Topic G — Place Value Strategies for Multiples of 10
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 왼쪽·오른쪽 어디든 같음 — 60 × 7 = 7 × 60
learn.content: 교환법칙으로 위치는 무관: 60 × 7 = 7 × 60 = 420. 10의 배수가 왼쪽이든 오른쪽이든 같은 답. 팁: 10의 배수를 항상 오른쪽으로 옮기고 표준 절차 적용. 예: 90 × 3 → 3 × 90 → 3 × 9 = 27 → 270. 흔한 실수 (M06): 60 × 7과 7 × 60을 다르게 푸는 학생 — 교환법칙으로 같은 답이라는 점을 항상 활용하세요.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.5 Lesson 5.4 'Multiplication Strategies for Multiples of 10' pp.201-204
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 3 Topic G — Place Value Strategies for Multiples of 10
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 10의 배수 곱셈 — 즉답 전략 4가지
learn.content: 전략 1 결합법칙: a × (b × 10) = (a × b) × 10. 전략 2 0 붙이기: 기본 사실 a × b 하고 0 한 개 첨부. 전략 3 자릿값 검산: 답이 (a × b)의 10배가 되는지 확인. 전략 4 분배법칙 (어려운 경우): 7 × 80 = (5 × 80) + (2 × 80) = 400+160 = 560. 🔍 검증: a × b0 형태에서 답은 항상 끝자리 0. 두 자리/세 자리 곱셈 결과 자릿수 점검.

hints: First compute the basic fact (drop the zero), then place the zero back.
A × (B × 10) = (A × B) × 10. Which choice follows this rule?

feedback.correct: Correct! The basic fact 5 × 8 = 40, then 5 × 80 = 40 tens = 400.
feedback.incorrect: Remember: find the basic fact first (5 × 8 = 40), then the zero makes it 40 tens = 400.

try.question: Which strategy correctly finds 5 × 80?
try.feedback_correct: Correct! The basic fact 5 × 8 = 40, then 5 × 80 = 40 tens = 400.

Step 2: subtract taken spaces from total: 540 − 287 = 253.

hints: Calculate each expression: multiply the non-zero digits, then place the zero.
4 × 5 = 20, so 4 × 50 = 200, not 240. That's the odd one out.

feedback.correct: Correct! 4 × 50 = 200, not 240. The others all equal 240.

practice_r3.question: Which expression does NOT equal 240?
practice_r3.feedback_correct: Correct! 4 × 50 = 200, not 240. The others all equal 240.

choices: No, because you cannot add partial products
hints: 200 + 150 = 350. Also check: basic fact 7 × 5 = 35, so 7 × 50 = 350. Jake is correct.

feedback.correct: Excellent! Jake used the Distributive Property: (4 + 3) × 50 = 200 + 150 = 350.

practice_r3.question: Jake knows 4 × 50 = 200 and 3 × 50 = 150. He says 7 × 50 = 350. Is Jake correct, and why?
practice_r3.feedback_correct: Excellent! Jake used the Distributive Property: (4 + 3) × 50 = 200 + 150 = 350.
